threads.verdict_notes: Generic suggestion from CR's training data that conflicts with the established local convention. Every other Sources/UI/*.swift in this repo uses pure alphabetical ordering (no framework/module grouping). Applying CR's patch made PresetMenu the only file with grouping. Reverted in e083b9d. If we want an import-grouping convention, it should be a project-wide decision applied uniformly, not a one-file change driven by a reviewer suggestion that doesn't check the rest of the codebase.

threads.finding_excerpt:
_🛠️ Refactor suggestion_ | _🟠 Major_ | _🏗️ Heavy lift_

**Keep `AppError` typed end-to-end.**

`CaptureError` stays structured, but `.graph(String)`, `.parameter(String)`, `.preset(String)`, `.engine(String)`, and `.persistence(String)` collapse the rest of the failure domain into UI-ready text. Tha
threads.verdict_notes: Tracked as U-011 in docs/decisions/uncertainty-log.md. Promoting .graph/.parameter/.preset/.engine/.persistence to typed payloads mirroring .capture(CaptureError) is the right model for V0.2. V0.1.0 collapse is acceptable because no consumer programmatically discriminates between the variants today; everything funnels into lastError + the debug-log panel.
